key_point: A viral social media post claims that eating garlic daily can prevent all types of cancer.
A viral social media post claims that 5G technology causes cancer and weakens the immune system.
A recent study claims that using essential oils can cure diabetes.
A recent study claims that using essential oils can cure asthma.
A viral social media post claims that drinking apple cider vinegar daily cures Type 2 Diabetes within 4 weeks.
A viral social media post claims that drinking green tea daily can cure Alzheimer's disease.
A viral social media post claims that drinking lemon water daily can cure cancer.
A recent study claims that taking vitamin C supplements can prevent the common cold.

A recent study published in the Journal of Gastroenterology claims that a specific strain of probiotics, Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG, can cure irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) in 70% of patients within six months.
A recent study claims that consuming 2 teaspoons of cinnamon daily can significantly lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels within two weeks.
A viral social media post claims that eating dark chocolate with at least 70% cocoa daily can completely prevent heart attacks.
A viral social media post claims that eating three bananas every day can prevent Alzheimer's disease due to their high potassium content.
A viral social media post claims that consuming 5 cloves of garlic daily for 30 days can completely eliminate high blood pressure without the need for medication.
A recent study from a German clinic claims that a weekly intake of 1000 mg of garlic extract significantly reduces the risk of developing common colds by 50%.
A health influencer claims that eating a clove of garlic every day for a month can cure high blood pressure without any side effects.
A viral social media post claims that taking a daily dose of 1000 mg of Vitamin C can prevent the common cold.
A recent online article claims that taking a daily dose of vitamin C supplements can prevent the common cold completely, based on the findings of a study conducted in New Zealand.
A recent article from an online health blog claims that consuming 10 grams of turmeric powder daily can completely eliminate the risk of developing Alzheimer's disease.
A recent viral YouTube video claims that consuming a teaspoon of turmeric every morning with warm water can cure chronic kidney disease.
A viral social media post claims that consuming 5 cloves of garlic daily for 30 days can completely eliminate high blood pressure without the need for medication.
A recent post on a health blog claims that eating a teaspoon of cinnamon every day can replace insulin treatment for Type 1 Diabetes patients.
A viral social media post claims that using a specific brand of essential oils can cure or prevent severe acute respiratory syndrome (SARS).

A recent YouTube video claims that the use of silver nanoparticles in water filtration systems can eliminate all bacteria and viruses, including the SARS-CoV-2 virus, from drinking water within seconds.
A recent blog post claims that consuming a teaspoon of apple cider vinegar before every meal can completely eliminate the need for insulin in individuals with Type 1 Diabetes within 6 weeks.
A viral social media post claims that consuming 2 tablespoons of flaxseed oil daily can completely cure eczema within 30 days for adults.
A recent article claims that taking a daily dose of 500 mg of vitamin C can completely prevent the common cold in adults.

A social media influencer claims that taking 1000 mg of Vitamin C daily for 2 weeks can prevent the common cold entirely, and this claim is backed by user comments and a purported study from an unnamed source.
